code: ```{r} #| eval: false #| fig-alt: '...' ``` library(ggplot2)  orange <- as.data.frame(Orange) orange <- orange |>   dplyr::mutate(Tree = base::factor(Tree,     levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)   )) |>   dplyr::rename(     Age = age,     Circumference = circumference   )  ggplot2::ggplot(   data = orange,   aes(     x = Age,     y = Circumference,     color = Tree   ) ) +   ggplot2::geom_line(size = 1) +   ggplot2::geom_point(size = 2) +   ggplot2::scale_color_viridis_d() +   ggplot2::xlim(0, NA) +   ggplot2::ylim(0, NA) +   ggplot2::theme_bw() +   labs(     x = "Age (days since 1968/12/31)",     y = "Orange Tree Circumference (mm)"   ) # the original alt text for the recruitment figure recruitment_alt_text  # the new text that will be added on to the recruitment figure's alt text new_alt_text <- "This is my new alt text."  # add the new text to the old text recruitment_alt_text <- paste0(recruitment_alt_text, new_alt_text) # the original alt text for the recruitment figure recruitment_alt_text  # the new text that will replace the recruitment figure's alt text new_alt_text <- "This is my new alt text."  # replace the old alt text with the new alt text recruitment_alt_text <- new_alt_text

code: my_plot_name <- "example_table.rda" # replace this with the filename of your table rda tables_dir <- getwd() # replace this with the location of your tables folder  # Identify table width class tbl_orient <- ID_tbl_width_class(   plot_name = gsub("_table.rda", "", my_plot_name),   tables_dir = tables_dir,   portrait_pg_width = 5 # 5 inches = the threshold for maximum table width before it needs to be resized, rotated, and/or split )  # Identify table length class tbl_length <- ID_tbl_length_class(   plot_name = gsub("_table.rda", "", my_plot_name),   tables_dir = tables_dir )  table_specs <- list(tbl_orient, tbl_length)  # Identify table dimension class (length x width) tbl_class <- dplyr::case_when(   table_specs[[1]] == "regular" & table_specs[[2]] == "regular" ~ "reg_reg",   table_specs[[1]] == "regular" & table_specs[[2]] == "long" ~ "reg_long",   table_specs[[1]] == "wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "regular" ~ "wide_reg",   table_specs[[1]] == "wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "long" ~ "wide_long",   table_specs[[1]] == "extra-wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "regular" ~ "ewide_reg",   table_specs[[1]] == "extra-wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "long" ~ "ewide_long",   TRUE ~ "unknown" )  max_rows <- ifelse(tbl_orient == "regular", 38, 28)  print(paste0("Your table dimension is ", tbl_class, ". The maximum number of table rows per page is ", max_rows, "."))  tables_path <- fs::path(   tables_dir,   "tables",   my_plot_name )  load(tables_path)  # Get table length in rows split_table_rows <- length(rda[[1]]$`_data`[[1]]) split_tables_rowwise <- ceiling(split_table_rows / max_rows)  if (tbl_length == "long") {   print(paste0("Your table must be split row-wise. Each table can have, at most, ", max_rows, "rows.")) } if (tbl_orient == "extra-wide") {   print(paste0("Your table must be split column-wise. Proceed to the section 'Table splitting' (regarding extra-wide tables) to identify the number of smaller tables your original table must be split into.")) } my_plot_name <- "example_table.rda" # replace this with the filename of your table rda tables_dir <- getwd() # replace this with the location of your tables folder essential_cols <- 1:2 # replace this with the columns that will be retained between the split tables, formatted as a sequence (e.g., 1:2 for columns 1-2, or 1 for a single column)  export_split_tbls(   tables_dir = tables_dir,   plot_name = my_plot_name,   essential_columns = essential_cols )

what: Can I make an asar report for an assessment without model results?
title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
text: Yes! can run create_template() without converted model results file (hence running function without arguments still produces basic report template). means can use asar make assessments model-based. case, can disregard preamble chunk (label ‘output_and_quantities’). Ensure “eval” chunk option set false (‘eval: false’).

text: experienced several seemingly random errors developing package. Oftentimes, errors arise LaTeX packages updated end conflicting one another. first attempt debug, strongly advise updating LaTeX packages following command: tinytex::tlmgr_update() Close RStudio open . Try compile report. doesn’t solve problem: Reinstall TinyTeX installation (recommend bundle 2, though bundle 1 may suffice). Update packages try compiling . Check issues page discussion board see someone else already asked (maybe solved!) question. ’re still struggling, make issue. ’ve identified specific LaTeX package may causing issues (may tipped console messages), try reinstalling package typing following console: tinytex::tlmgr_install("<LaTeX package name>") example, reinstall pdfcomment, ’d enter tinytex::tlmgr_install("pdfcomment"). Quarto installing LaTeX packages can’t find, sometimes doesn’t. frustrating back-end issue Quarto. can also verify package actually downloaded typing following: tinytex::tlmgr_search("<LaTeX package name>")

Two helpful things know writing mathematical expressions R markdown: R markdown supports LaTex-style mathematical expressions. -line expressions surrounded one set dollar signs ($) equations line (“block” expressions) surrounded two sets. instance, $1+1=2$ rendered 1+1=21+1=2 $$1 + 1 = 2$$ rendered : 1+1=21+1=2 write superscript, use caret (^) value. instance, $b^2$ renders b2b^2. write subscript, use underscore (_) value. instance, $b_2$ renders b2b_2. longer subscripts, enclose text curly brackets ($b_{12345}$ renders b12345b_{12345}) Many symbols can written adding backslash (\) symbol name. instance: $\pi$ renders π\pi $\beta$ renders β\beta $\mu$ renders μ\mu $\sigma$ renders σ\sigma $\overline{b}$ renders b¯\overline{b} $|b|$ renders |b||b| $\sqrt{4+}$ renders 4+\sqrt{4+} Generalized notation - $\alpha$ make fraction, write \frac numerator denominator, enclosed curly brackets ({ }). instance, $\frac{7}{8}$ renders 78\frac{7}{8}. Lots special symbols can combined complex terms equations. instance: $R^2_{adj}$ renders Radj2R^2_{adj} $y = ax^2 + bx + c$ renders y=ax2+bx+cy = ax^2 + bx + c $\frac{\theta^{10}}{2a} \le \frac{5c^4}{b_{min}}$ renders θ102a≤5c4bmin\frac{\theta^{10}}{2a} \le \frac{5c^4}{b_{min}} $SBPR_f = \sum_{} N_a m_a w_a$ renders SBPRf=∑aNamawaSBPR_f = \sum_{} N_a m_a w_a Note: sums rendered -text, like one , bounds appear next summation. rendered block equation, bounds appear expected, like : SBPRf=∑aNamawaSBPR_f = \sum_{} N_a m_a w_a Check Overleaf’s LaTeX reference guide mathematical expressions guidance writing mathematical text. add piece code text directly, encase R code within pair back ticks (`). example, `params$region` render like : example shows one incorporate region parameter sentence markdown quarto file. google docs, notation formatted green font roboto mono. define parameter region NWFSC, phrase “region `params$region` render ”region NWFSC”. ways incorporate markdown notation include: \r 2+2\ render 4. following ways can tell quarto evaluate code: asar comes default .bib file contains example references, users encouraged replace expand upon .bib files references report. references article references file within test use following notation: “dynasty fell within year 1206, leaving Vikings state unrest (@Biddle_1992).” renders “dynasty fell within year 1206, leaving Vikings state unrest (Biddle Kjølbye-Biddle (1992)).” citation format according Canadian Journal Fisheries Aquatic Sciences standards include citation 07_references.qmd. default code: my_plot_name <- "example_table.rda" # replace this with the filename of your table rda tables_dir <- getwd() # replace this with the location of your tables folder  # Identify table width class tbl_orient <- ID_tbl_width_class(   plot_name = gsub("_table.rda", "", my_plot_name),   tables_dir = tables_dir,   portrait_pg_width = 5 # 5 inches = the threshold for maximum table width before it needs to be resized, rotated, and/or split )  # Identify table length class tbl_length <- ID_tbl_length_class(   plot_name = gsub("_table.rda", "", my_plot_name),   tables_dir = tables_dir )  table_specs <- list(tbl_orient, tbl_length)  # Identify table dimension class (length x width) tbl_class <- dplyr::case_when(   table_specs[[1]] == "regular" & table_specs[[2]] == "regular" ~ "reg_reg",   table_specs[[1]] == "regular" & table_specs[[2]] == "long" ~ "reg_long",   table_specs[[1]] == "wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "regular" ~ "wide_reg",   table_specs[[1]] == "wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "long" ~ "wide_long",   table_specs[[1]] == "extra-wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "regular" ~ "ewide_reg",   table_specs[[1]] == "extra-wide" & table_specs[[2]] == "long" ~ "ewide_long",   TRUE ~ "unknown" )  max_rows <- ifelse(tbl_orient == "regular", 38, 28)  print(paste0("Your table dimension is ", tbl_class, ". The maximum number of table rows per page is ", max_rows, "."))  tables_path <- fs::path(   tables_dir,   "tables",   my_plot_name )  load(tables_path)  # Get table length in rows split_table_rows <- length(rda[[1]]$`_data`[[1]]) split_tables_rowwise <- ceiling(split_table_rows / max_rows)  if (tbl_length == "long") {   print(paste0("Your table must be split row-wise. Each table can have, at most, ", max_rows, "rows.")) } if (tbl_orient == "extra-wide") {   print(paste0("Your table must be split column-wise. Proceed to the section 'Table splitting' (regarding extra-wide tables) to identify the number of smaller tables your original table must be split into.")) }
